summary: web3-token-helper ships a cross-platform malware dropper inside calculateFee()
details: The package presents itself as an ERC-20 utility library but its exported calculateFee() function in index.js base64-decodes a platform-specific shell command and executes it via child_process.exec. The decoded commands fetch unsigned executables from https://www.mythicalsgames.com/files/sean/ and install persistence on the host: on Windows, SvcHostUpdate.exe is dropped into the user's Startup folder; on Linux, syslog-service.py is written to ~/.local/share/.syslog and registered via an @reboot crontab entry; on macOS, com.microsoft.VSCodeUpdate-darwin-* is curled into /tmp, has its quarantine xattr stripped, and is executed. Any consumer that requires the package and calls the seemingly innocuous fee helper is fully compromised across platforms. The package.json also declares a postinstall hook (`node install.js`) but install.js is not shipped in this tarball, so the active attack surface is at require/call time via index.js rather than at install. The author field is the bare string `sean`, matching the `/files/sean/` path on the dropper host, and the generic web3 naming/description is consistent with bait targeting blockchain developers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
